author: ghost
text:
In paper, You wrote that you will use rates [6, 12,24] Atrous rates as you might see in the image below. But [your code](https://github.com/zhanghang1989/PyTorch-Encoding/blob/master/encoding/models/sseg/deeplab.py#L61) defaults as [12,24,36]. I wonder why they are different. [Here](https://pytorch.org/docs/master/generated/torch.nn.Conv2d.html) is the Pytorch's conv2d dilation definition.  
[![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/50353282/88880054-c57ca980-d266-11ea-8147-55bb5c030633.png)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/50353282/88880054-c57ca980-d266-11ea-8147-55bb5c030633.png)

author: lxtGH
text: [@Heonjin](https://github.com/Heonjin) If the network output stride is 16, we use rates [6, 12,24] . If the network output stride is 8, we use rates [12,24,36].
